# Quoin Buildings

> Quoin is the AI Operating Partner for vertically integrated real estate companies. Discovery, governed implementation, and managed operations under one method.

Quoin makes real estate companies queryable, governable, and agent-ready, then builds and operates the agents that run inside their workflows. Quoin runs three contractually distinct service lines: an Operating Diagnostic that decides whether AI belongs, an Operating Implementation that builds the Operating Intelligence Platform and the agents on top of it, and Managed AgentOps that operates them under continuous evals, change control, incident response, and lifecycle management.

The defensible thesis: most enterprises do not have an AI-agent problem first; they have an operating intelligence problem first. Critical truth lives in spreadsheets, macros, manual adjustments, and expert memory. Agents that improvise over fragile truth fail. Quoin maps how truth is actually produced before any agent is built.

## Who this is for

- Senior leadership at vertically integrated real estate companies
- REITs and large private real estate companies
- Firms with regional operating layers and multi-system complexity
- Technology, security, and risk leaders who must co-approve AI work
- Leadership teams where AI needs to affect operating capacity, reporting quality, risk control, and portfolio performance

## Who this is not for

- Pure asset managers with no operating control
- Pure third-party property managers with no asset-side authority
- Companies looking only for a chatbot
- Teams that want AI demos without changing workflows, controls, or operating ownership

## Core Model

The five operating phases:

1. Map the workflow: systems, roles, exceptions, approvals, source trust, and value.
2. Build the Operating Intelligence Platform: source inventory, canonical entities, semantic and truth layer, document and knowledge layer, permissions, observability.
3. Build the agent: agents, automations, connectors, review queues, evals, audit trails, operating interfaces.
4. Deploy with controls: permitted actions, prohibited actions, human review, access boundaries, escalation, logging.
5. Manage continuously under Managed AgentOps: quality, drift, overrides, incidents, adoption, change control, expansion or retirement.

## Three Offerings

- Operating Diagnostic: pre-build engagement that decides what AI belongs in the workflow. Outputs a decision packet and managed lifecycle object. No production credentials required.
- Operating Implementation: post-approval build of the Operating Intelligence Platform's first wedge (a Minimum Viable Foundry) and the agents on top of it.
- Managed AgentOps: recurring operation of deployed agents with evals, source freshness, change control, incident response, and lifecycle reviews.

## Core Pages

- [Home](https://quoinbuildings.com/): Positioning, evidence, operating principles, three offerings, value case, method, fit.
- [Method](https://quoinbuildings.com/method): The five operating phases plus the seven-umbrella detail and the five-touchpoint client experience.
- [Platform](https://quoinbuildings.com/platform): Operating Intelligence Platform architecture, Minimum Viable Foundry, staged data access, REIT example queries, complementary stack.
- [Diagnostic](https://quoinbuildings.com/diagnostic): Operating Diagnostic offering — Section 1 pre-build engagement.
- [Implementation](https://quoinbuildings.com/implementation): Operating Implementation offering — Section 2 governed build.
- [AgentOps](https://quoinbuildings.com/agentops): Managed AgentOps offering — recurring operations.
- [Technology Leaders](https://quoinbuildings.com/technology-leaders): For CTOs, CIOs, CISOs, CDOs, and enterprise architects who must co-approve AI work.
- [Concepts](https://quoinbuildings.com/concepts): Truth profile taxonomy, platform layers, Minimum Viable Foundry, agent safety ladder, readiness paths.
- [Perspectives](https://quoinbuildings.com/perspectives): Long-form articles on operating intelligence, governance, and the gap between AI capability and enterprise readiness.
- [Team](https://quoinbuildings.com/team): Quoin team, roles, institutional experience.
- [Contact](https://quoinbuildings.com/contact): Request a 30-minute call.

## Best Summary

Quoin runs an AI Operating Partner method built around one observation: AI fails inside enterprises because the underlying truth is fragile, not because the model is weak. Quoin's first job is to make the company queryable through governed sources, canonical entities, semantic definitions, and explicit truth profiles. Only then are agents built on top. After launch, Managed AgentOps keeps the agents safe, useful, and aligned as workflows, systems, policies, portfolios, and risk posture change.
